title: Declaration of Policies
content:
(a) The State shall uphold the dignity of its citizens, especially Filipino migrant workers, monitor international conventions, adopt and ratify protections for migrant workers, and enter into bilateral agreements with countries receiving overseas Filipino workers.

(b) The State shall provide full protection to labor, promote full employment and equality of employment opportunities, and provide adequate social, economic, and legal services to Filipino migrant workers.

(c) The State recognizes the contribution of Filipino migrant workers to the economy but does not promote overseas employment as a means for economic growth; it shall create local employment opportunities and promote equitable wealth distribution.

(d) The State affirms equality before the law of women and men, recognizes the role and vulnerabilities of overseas migrant women workers, and shall apply gender-sensitive criteria in policies and programs affecting migrant workers.

(e) Free access to courts and legal assistance shall not be denied due to poverty; mechanisms shall be instituted to protect the rights and interests of distressed overseas Filipinos, including documented and undocumented migrant workers.

(f) The right of Filipino migrant workers and overseas Filipinos to participate in democratic decision-making and be represented in relevant institutions is recognized and guaranteed.

(g) The State shall expand access to free skills development and enhancement programs for migrant workers and shall deploy only skilled Filipino workers as soon as practicable.

(h) The State recognizes NGOs, trade unions, workers associations, and recruitment and manning agencies as partners in protecting and promoting the welfare of Filipino migrant workers and shall cooperate with them in trust and mutual respect.

metadata.source_file: Omnibus IRR of RA 8042 as amended by RA 10022.pdf
